topic.what: Comprehensive overview of Traditional Chinese Medicine approaches to H. pylori-associated gastritis, covering pattern differentiation (spleen-stomach damp-heat, liver-stomach disharmony) and classical formula-based treatment strategies.
topic.how: Systematic narrative review synthesizing TCM diagnostic frameworks, dominant syndrome patterns, representative formulas (e.g., Huang Lian Jie Du Tang, Chai Hu Shu Gan San), and integrative combination with standard triple/quadruple therapy.
topic.why_matters: H. pylori eradication rates with conventional antibiotics are declining due to resistance; TCM offers adjunctive and synergistic strategies that may improve eradication, reduce side-effects, and address underlying mucosal dysfunction through pattern-based individualisation.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Breadth of TCM syndrome patterns discussed in relation to H. pylori gastritis
characteristics.value: ≥5 distinct patterns reviewed including spleen-stomach damp-heat, liver-stomach disharmony, stomach yin deficiency, spleen-stomach qi deficiency, blood stasis
characteristics.evidence: Narrative review of primary TCM clinical and experimental literature
characteristics.context: Pattern identification is the cornerstone of TCM prescribing and determines formula selection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Quality of evidence for combining TCM formulas with standard H. pylori eradication regimens
characteristics.value: Moderate — predominantly small RCTs and meta-analyses with heterogeneous outcomes
characteristics.evidence: Synthesis of published RCTs and meta-analyses cited in review
characteristics.context: Evidence quality limited by small sample sizes and methodological variability across included studies

good_for: Clinicians seeking a structured TCM framework for H. pylori gastritis management
Researchers designing integrative trials combining TCM with antibiotic eradication
KB articles on pattern differentiation logic and formula mapping
not_for: Mechanistic molecular detail on individual herbs
Head-to-head efficacy comparisons between specific formulas
requires: Access to original cited RCTs for quantitative evidence appraisal

novelty: Integrates TCM pattern-differentiation taxonomy with H. pylori microbiology in a single structured review
Maps specific classical formulas to each syndrome pattern, providing a prescribing reference aligned with modern diagnosis
open_problems: Lack of large, well-powered RCTs with standardised TCM diagnosis criteria
Mechanistic pathways by which formula components modulate H. pylori virulence or host immunity remain incompletely characterised
No consensus on optimal integration timing (concurrent vs. sequential with antibiotics)
